title: Protocol for Dephosphorylation of 5′ ends of DNA using Quick CIP (NEB #M0525)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"edc9v","text":"Quick CIP is a heat-labile version of calf intestinal alkaline phosphatase (CIP) purified from a recombinant source.","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"e83vi","text":"Rapid and irreversible heat inactivation eliminates unwanted activity","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"aan1r","text":"Improved storage stability versus native enzyme","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ct90b","text":"Faster reaction setup (no supplemental additives like zinc required) and shorter incubation time","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"f7u1j","text":"Flexible reaction conditions (active in any restriction enzyme buffer, no clean-up required)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"d7ltp","text":"Less enzyme required (high specific activity), resulting in a lower cost per reaction","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"aprhr","text":"No need for multiple phosphatases (Quick CIP removes 5′- and 3′- phosphates from DNA, RNA and dNTPs )","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bcab5","text":"Active on unincorporated dNTPs in PCR products - improves DNA sequencing and SNP analysis","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"d8f2j","text":"Recombinant for purity, consistency and value","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"e5tca","text":"Dephosphorylation of DNA 5′-ends using Quick CIP in a Restriction Enzyme Reaction","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8ao4s","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ae258","text":"The phosphatase can be added directly into the digestion reaction during or after DNA digestion","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fnmof","text":"Add    of Quick CIP for every 1 pmol of DNA ends (about   of a 3 kb plasmid) and incubate at    for 10 minutes","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":4,"length":1},{"key":1,"offset":56,"length":1},{"key":2,"offset":93,"length":1}],"data":[]},{"key":"7o5ra","text":"Quick CIP is active in all NEB restriction enzyme buffers","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"desh6","text":"The restriction enzyme should be heat inactivated at the same time as the phosphatase after digest and dephosphorylation","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4v6r7","text":"If restriction enzyme(s) cannot be heat inactivated, DNA purification is required before ligation","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5ps20","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6n8us","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"e7col","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5d086","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"amount","mutability":"","data":{"amount":"1","guid":"56779EB0FB5611E9A1363FED7B041AF9","id":0,"label":"","unit":1}},"1":{"type":"amount","mutability":"","data":{"amount":"1","guid":"C726F890FB5611E9A1363FED7B041AF9","id":0,"label":"","unit":4}},"2":{"type":"temperature","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"EEEB5470FB5611E9A1363FED7B041AF9","id":0,"label":"","temperature":"37","unit":10}}}}

steps: Prepare a 20 µL reaction as follows: %s %s DNA1 pmol of DNA ends*CutSmart® Buffer (10X)2 μlQuick CIP1 μlH2O, purifiedto 20 μl** * Note: 1 pmol of DNA ends is about 1 μg of a 3 kb plasmid. ** Scale larger reaction volumes proportionally.
Stop reaction by heat-inactivation at 80 °C for 00:02:00 .
creator: New England   Biolabs
